title: Fail to build on Intel Macs with descriptive error
body:
We've recently dropped Intel Mac support from the repo, and we no longer build on that platform. Unfortunately, it's not at all clear why when it happens - just a generic "all Go files in directory excluded by build constraints" message. This is a terrible user experience for folks on Intel Mac - we should be throwing a descriptive error indicating that Intel Mac support has been removed in Podman 6..

This should also be added to `CONTRIBUTING.md` to notify prospective contributors that their setup won't work.

comments.body: how did you attempt to build it ? in the makefile ?

i would think that it would be the same on riscv or other platforms ? its kind of a go thing no ?  and we have other problems in the makefile we've ignored like `make` on darwin or windows. you have to know to build the remote client.

Yeah, straight Makefile build.

Honestly, our supported architectures list is pretty limited - Linux + everything, Windows + AMD64/ARM64, Mac + ARM64, FreeBSD + (I presume all supported architectures?). That's not prohibitively difficult to add checks for in the Makefile?
Working on this. Adding a $(error) check in the Makefile for darwin/amd64 and a note in CONTRIBUTING.md.
